quotes.verbatim_quote:
The student must remember to relate each Dorian Mede to its parent key before attempting to ap-
ply the numerical formulas for the formation of the four types of minor chords and before attempting
to play the scale (dorian mode) of these chords.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is the method's core procedural rule for all minor chord work: always derive the Dorian mode from its parent major key before applying any formula.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Tones No. 1-3-5-7 of the B Dorian Mode form the B Minor Seventh Chord.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines the numerical formula for building a minor seventh chord from the Dorian mode.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Tones No. 1-3-5-7-9 of the B Dorian Mode form the B Minor Ninth Chord.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Extends the chord-building formula to the ninth, showing how adding one scale degree transforms a minor seventh into a minor ninth chord.

quotes.topic: Shared triad across minor chord types
quotes.verbatim_quote:
Note that all four chords contain the same triad, B, D, F-Sharp; and a// four chords use the same
basic scale, the B Dorian Mode, to establish their sound.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Establishes the unifying principle that all four minor chord types share a common triad and parent scale.
